ops.title: daemonkit: cap concurrent codex fan-outs at ~2 lanes

ops.body:
**Operational lesson (2026-07-18, this host):** a 5-way concurrent codex xhigh fan-out (the first P1 core-port attempt) wedged and OOM'd — two lanes hung frozen mid-`ccx code read`, two were SIGKILLed at exit 137. Cap concurrent codex lanes at ~2, and instruct codex review lanes to use plain `diff`/`cat` rather than ccx tooling (the ccx MCP calls were where lanes wedged).

**Codex quota:** was exhausted mid-run but the user reset it (2026-07-18) — codex/gpt-5.6-sol is available again, route per the CLAUDE.md Models table normally. During the outage window P1's sol finder pass was substituted with opus xhigh same-tier review (recorded in that phase's cc-notes criteria); that substitution stands as-verified, a codex re-review is optional not required.
